close_reason: Unavoidable: reqwest::RequestBuilder::bearer_auth copies the token into a HeaderValue internally. Cannot use Zeroizing in reqwest headers. Already documented in the crate's Zeroization doc section.
description:
src/lib.rs:263, 371 'self.client.get(...).bearer_auth(self.access_token.as_str())'. reqwest internally formats 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' and stores the header value in a non-Zeroizing http::HeaderValue (Bytes-backed). The header value lives in the Request object and any retained Response state until dropped, none of which zeroes on drop.

Per request, the access token is copied (formatted with 'Bearer ' prefix) into reqwest-internal heap. The crate's stated zeroization guarantee in the lib-level doc comment (lines 49-58) does not cover this copy. The doc comment partially acknowledges the equivalent gap for the response body but not for the request header.

Cross-crate aspect: same pattern in secretx-doppler:137 and secretx-hashicorp-vault:197 (vault uses X-Vault-Token, same root issue: HeaderValue is not zeroizing). The gap is unavoidable at the reqwest layer; mitigation requires either a custom http::HeaderValue with a Zeroize-aware Bytes backing, or accepting the gap and documenting it explicitly.

Suggested fix: extend the zeroization doc comment in src/lib.rs:48-58 to enumerate the bearer_auth header copy alongside the existing 'raw HTTP response buffer' acknowledgment. This is documentation, not code change — but the gap should be explicit.
title: bearer_auth header value is non-Zeroizing copy of access token
